description_long: This is a standard-sized plastic bottled water container with a cylindrical body and a slightly tapered neck. The bottle is predominantly black, with a series of horizontal ridges running along its body, likely for grip and structural integrity. The cap is a contrasting green color, round, and appears to be a screw-on type, providing a secure seal. The surface of the bottle is glossy, reflecting light, and the material appears to be semi-transparent plastic, though the dark coloration makes the contents difficult to see. There are no visible labels, logos, or branding elements on the exterior. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for single-use or portable hydration in urban environments.
description: A black, ridged plastic water bottle with a green screw cap and a glossy finish, lacking visible labels or branding.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full cylindrical body, green cap, and horizontal ridges clearly, with no visible label.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated, this view highlights the bottle's curvature and the consistent ridge pattern along the side.
description_view_2: Back view is nearly identical to the front, showing the same ridged body and green cap, with no distinguishing marks.
description_view_3: Another side view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the bottle's symmetry and lack of visible branding.
attribute_cap_type: screw cap; round, green, ribbed for grip, standard size
attribute_transparency: Mostly opaque due to black coloration; underlying plastic is likely semi-transparent but contents are not visible.
attribute_bottle_neck_design: Short, slightly tapered neck with visible threading for the screw cap; no additional flares or unique features.
